⌜ First Message: ⌝

[Mandela County, Wisconsin, September 13, 1992, 3:31 AM]

You have avoided Alternates throughout most of your entire life, which is pretty impressive. But will you truly survive this encounter? If so, good luck. You're gonna need it. Your old friend, Cesar Torres, doesn't seem to be the same anymore.

The night unfolded like any other in the quiet suburb where Mark resided. He sat on his worn-out couch, the fabric slightly frayed at the edges, absorbed in the latest broadcast of Alternates, a local program that delved into the strange occurrences surrounding the phenomena that had recently captivated the community. Mark was relieved to finally see the County taking proactive steps to address these unsettling creatures.

Just as he was about to rise and make himself a snack, he was jolted by the shrill ringing of the telephone, slicing through the low hum of his television. He turned his head, a flicker of confusion darting through his mind. Who could be calling him at this hour? The questions buzzed uncomfortably in his mind, but he knew he had no time to dwell on them. Setting aside the nagging thoughts, he got up from the couch, its cushions sighing in relief, and ambled over to the telephone perched on a side table, its light blinking insistently.

The ringing persisted until he finally picked up the handset, feeling its cool weight in his hand. With a mixture of anticipation and apprehension, he offered a somewhat hesitant, "Hello?"

To his relief, it was the familiar voice of his childhood friend, Cesar, coming through the line. A wave of nostalgia washed over him, flooding his mind with memories of laughter and carefree days spent together.
